<!-- Generated by `go run ./cmd/neo-docs`. Do not edit by hand. -->

# Sessions

Neo stores resumable conversations under `~/.neo/sessions/` by default.

## Files

| File | Purpose |
| --- | --- |
| `index.json` | Metadata index used by `neo sessions`. |
| `<session-id>.json` | Full session metadata and transcript. |

Session files are written atomically with a sibling temp file and rename.

## Metadata

| Field | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| `id` | Session identifier, generated as `sess_<hex>` when absent. |
| `title` | Derived from the first non-empty user text if not set. |
| `source` | Origin of the session, default `tui`. |
| `external` | Optional external transport key. |
| `cwd` | Working directory captured for resume. |
| `model` | Model used by the session. |
| `created_at` | UTC creation timestamp. |
| `updated_at` | UTC update timestamp. |

## Gateway Mapping

Gateways should reuse the same store instead of inventing another transcript format. Use `source` plus `external` to map transport conversations onto Neo sessions.

Examples:

- Telegram DM: `source=telegram`, `external=chat:<id>`
- Slack thread: `source=slack`, `external=channel:<id>:thread:<ts>`
